channel.title: d3 on Cameron DeCoster
channel.description: Recent content in d3 on Cameron DeCoster

item.title: One and a Half Years In
item.description: I have some professional news: I’m starting a new job as a Software Developer at Observable! My last day at Markit was on 05/12 and my first day at Observable is on 05/16. How did this turn in my career path come to be? Let me recount it for you. But don’t worry; there’s no bad news in this story. I started working at the Markit Digital division of IHS Markit in October of 2020 (now a division in S&P Global; yay mergers!

item.title: Testing D3 Format Specifiers
item.description: The D3 library provides a number of modules that can be used to format data, including d3-format and d3-time-format. These are very useful because they can take a JavaScript value and make it more human readable. These two modules work as follows: Provide a value Provide a format specifier The module returns a formatted version of that value based on the format specifier In my case, I have a configuration option in a project where users can pass in a specifier string which will control how some labels are displayed.

item.title: Calculate the Zoom Transform Between Two Scales in D3
item.description: D3 (data-driven documents) is a JavaScript library for creating and interacting with data visualizations. Think of charts, scatter plots, heatmaps, and bubble graphs just to name a few. Anything and everything required to create a data visualization is included in the D3 library. For my day job (Markit Digital, come join me!), I develop a number of charting products, including a JavaScript based interactive time-series chart which uses D3 Scale, D3 Selection, and D3 Zoom.
